title: NavigationEventArgs Object
description: The NavigationEventArgs provides access to the information sent from the browser within a palette on a navigation event.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: browserCommandInput
properties.description: When the event is fired from a BrowserCommandInput object, this property returns the specific BrowserCommandInput that caused the event to fire. In all other cases this property returns null.

properties.name: firingEvent
properties.description: The event that the firing is in response to.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: launchExternally
properties.description: If True, the URL will be navigated to in an external browser by the operating system. If False, the default value, the URL will be navigated to in the palette's browser.

properties.name: navigationURL
properties.description: The URL that is being navigated to.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: preventDefault
properties.description: If True, the default handling of this navigation event will not continue. If False, the default value, the default handling of this navigation event will continue.

browserCommandInput.title: NavigationEventArgs.browserCommandInput Property
browserCommandInput.description: When the event is fired from a BrowserCommandInput object, this property returns the specific BrowserCommandInput that caused the event to fire. In all other cases this property returns null.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = navigationEventArgs_var.browserCommandInput

firingEvent.title: NavigationEventArgs.firingEvent Property
firingEvent.description: The event that the firing is in response to.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = navigationEventArgs_var.firingEvent

isValid.title: NavigationEventArgs.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = navigationEventArgs_var.isValid

launchExternally.title: NavigationEventArgs.launchExternally Property
launchExternally.description: If True, the URL will be navigated to in an external browser by the operating system. If False, the default value, the URL will be navigated to in the palette's browser.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = navigationEventArgs_var.launchExternally

# Set the value of the property.
navigationEventArgs_var.launchExternally = propertyValue

navigationURL.title: NavigationEventArgs.navigationURL Property
navigationURL.description: The URL that is being navigated to.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = navigationEventArgs_var.navigationURL

# Set the value of the property.
navigationEventArgs_var.navigationURL = propertyValue

# Get the value of the property.

preventDefault.description: If True, the default handling of this navigation event will not continue. If False, the default value, the default handling of this navigation event will continue.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = navigationEventArgs_var.preventDefault

# Set the value of the property.

classType.title: NavigationEventArgs.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.
